bead.title: routing P1: ddx applies shared failure-classification table; single-owner one-hop middle->max escalation
bead.description: Implement the ddx side of the FEAT-004 failure-classification table: ddx work maps each attempt outcome to exactly the action in the addendum table (success/already_satisfied=close; no_changes=close/unclaim NO tier-escalation; genuine impl/capability failure=escalate middle->max ONCE; dirty=clean+same-tier retry; transport=same-tier reroute; quota-exhausted=global pool cooldown; auth/setup=operator-attention; timeout=clean+retry then operator-attention; merge-conflict=unclaim+retry; max genuine failure=operator-attention). ddx owns the ONE middle->max tier escalation (per the escalation-owner decision bead fizeau-994604ec); a per-pool in-flight max-attempt cap prevents multi-worker stampede. Depends on the escalation-owner contract + the shared classifier (fizeau-44e98499).
bead.acceptance: 1. ddx classifies each outcome per the addendum table (table-driven test). 2. Exactly one middle->max escalation, only on typed genuine failure; no_changes/dirty/transport/quota/auth do NOT escalate. 3. Per-pool in-flight max-attempt cap enforced (test simulates concurrent genuine failures, asserts cap). 4. quota-exhausted -> global pool cooldown, not N per-bead mutations (test). 5. cd /Users/erik/Projects/ddx/cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green. 6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

{"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"system_unready: Three blockers prevent a cold sub-agent from executing. (1) 'FEAT-004 failure-classification table' is ambiguous: in ddx, FEAT-004 is the Beads spec (docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md), which contains no failure-classification table or addendum. AC item 1 directs the agent to implement per 'the addendum table', pointing to an artifact that cannot be found in this repo. The intended reference appears to be fizeau's FEAT-004, which is inaccessible from ddx. The canonical ddx routing outcome table is TD-031 §3.2 (outcome → label/event/extra mapping), whose vocabulary diverges materially from the inline mappings in this bead (e.g. 'dirty', 'transport', 'quota-exhausted' vs TD-031's 'review_error', 'transient infra/quota/transport', 'paused-infra'). (2) fizeau-44e98499 is declared a hard dependency ('Depends on … the shared classifier (fizeau-44e98499)'), but whether this is a Go package import — requiring a stable interface before ddx can compile — or a design-alignment-only reference is unresolvable without the fizeau repo. If code-level, the interface contract must be finalized and tagged before claim. (3) The 'per-pool in-flight max-attempt cap' in AC item 3 is a new concurrency primitive not described in TD-031 or any accessible ddx design document; scope, data structure, and storage carrier are undefined.","fingerprint":"83c84f7dbb894f47fce4d2c1cb8eeb6e5ddc4c8ac7f4e19a4fabbea78914fc39","policy_mode":"warn-only","reason":"system_unready","rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.system_unready","suggested_action":"revise the rewrite so it preserves every explicit commitment","warning_fingerprint":"83c84f7dbb894f47fce4d2c1cb8eeb6e5ddc4c8ac7f4e19a4fabbea78914fc39"}
